title: Shaders, FLIP, Wave, Iridescent & Corners
description: WebGL-style scroll reveal, FLIP grid morph, dual-wave scroll text, iridescent cursor buttons, and the new CSS corner-shape property.

drops.title: Scroll-Reveal Shader Gallery
drops.description: Canvas wipe with distorted wavy edge — mimics a GLSL uProgress shader reveal driven by IntersectionObserver.
drops.concept: As each artwork enters the viewport, a progress value (0→1) drives a canvas mask that wipes the image from bottom to top with a sine-wave distorted edge. An SVG feTurbulence filter adds displacement distortion, replicating the cinematic shader-reveal feel from Codrops' Three.js tutorial without any WebGL dependencies.

drops.title: FLIP Grid Morph
drops.description: Three-line FLIP pattern: capture bounding rects, switch CSS Grid class, animate items from old positions to new — zero coordinate math.
drops.concept: FLIP (First, Last, Invert, Play) is a technique popularized by GSAP that animates real CSS layout reflow. You capture where items currently are, apply the new layout class instantly, then use Web Animations API to play from the captured 'old' positions to the natural 'new' positions. Items visually morph between grid configurations including 3×3, featured, magazine, and 4-column layouts.
drops.source: Codrops — Animating Responsive Grid Layout Transitions with GSAP Flip (Jan 2026)

interaction.click: Click layout buttons to trigger grid morphing animation

drops.title: Dual-Wave Scroll Text
drops.description: A single sine formula drives two columns in perfect opposition — left column waves forward, right waves backward as you scroll.
drops.concept: Both text columns share one sine-wave formula keyed to scroll progress. The left column uses a +1 multiplier, the right uses −1, making them oscillate in mathematical opposition. Each word's position in the wave is offset by its index multiplied by waveNumber, distributing words across the sine curve so they ripple continuously rather than moving as a block.
drops.source: Codrops / Valentin Descombes — Dual-Wave Text Animation (Jan 2026)

interaction.mouse: Scroll the page to drive the wave amplitude and phase

drops.title: Mouse-Reactive Iridescent Button
drops.description: Pointer offset writes to CSS custom properties; a wide-gamut conic-gradient glow physically follows your cursor across the button.
drops.concept: A pointermove listener calculates cursor distance from the button center and writes it into --cx and --cy CSS custom properties. The ::after pseudo-element — a blurred, saturated conic-gradient using OKLCH wide-gamut colors — translates to those coordinates, making a holographic rainbow light source appear to follow the cursor. The 0.12s ease-out transition gives the glow physical lag that makes it feel tangible.

interaction.mouse: Hover over any button to reveal the holo glow — move cursor across to see it track

drops.title: CSS corner-shape UI Kit
drops.description: Chrome 139+ ships corner-shape: squircle | bevel | notch | scoop — native iOS curves with working box-shadow. Animate corner geometry on hover.
drops.concept: The new CSS corner-shape property pairs with border-radius to produce squircle, bevel, notch, and scoop corners natively — no SVG masks, no clip-path hacks — while keeping box-shadow, border, and outline working normally. Cards and buttons can transition their corner shape with `transition: corner-shape 0.4s`, morphing geometry as a micro-interaction. A complete UI kit (cards, buttons, pill badges) showcases all shapes with live switching and graceful clip-path fallback.

interaction.click: Click shape buttons to morph corner geometry on all cards and buttons simultaneously
interaction.mouse: Hover cards and buttons to see border glow
